title: Fragrant Smoky Beef Binchotan Binchotan-Grilled Cutlet Bowl
description: A smoky, juicy, and cozy Peruvian dish featuring beef cutlet, yuzu-cure, and a binchotan-grill vessel.
instructions: Prepare the yuzu-cure by mixing yuzu juice, soy sauce, brown sugar, black pepper, and salt in a small bowl.
Rub the beef cutlet with yuzu-cure, making sure to coat it evenly.
Preheat the binchotan-grill to medium-high heat.
Sear the beef cutlet for 2-3 minutes per side, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 130°F (54°C).
Transfer the beef cutlet to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Meanwhile, toast the sesame seeds by heating a small skillet over medium heat until fragrant.
Add the pickled mustard seeds to the skillet and cook for another minute, stirring frequently.
To assemble the dish, place the sliced red onion and chopped cilantro on a plate, followed by the seared beef cutlet, toasted sesame seeds, and pickled mustard seeds.
Drizzle with additional yuzu juice if desired.
Garnish with additional yuzu-cure if desired.
